directive_events.owner_directive: Create a concise, user-oriented architecture map with the same 15 top-level sections as the canonical architecture reference; link it from the user-guide index. Rely on the existing site staging and src-triggered Pages workflow for deployment.

Remove Scope and sections 11, 13, 14, and 15; keep section 12; explain the six basic data types in a table, explain the basic block types in a table, and use a table in section 12 to show what users can extend.
directive_events.reason: Owner requested a substantial content restructure after reviewing the first draft.

directive_events.owner_directive: In section 4, split the closing explanation into canonical zone and inheritance, and add canonical-zone storage formats to the six-type table using the architecture document. Remove Custom block as a block type; replace the engine-state closing sentence with block extensibility. Expand Plot System with an architecture table. Remove not-but and similar contrastive negative phrasing throughout; use direct declarative statements.
directive_events.reason: Owner refined the data, block, plot, and writing-style requirements after reviewing the second draft.

directive_events.owner_directive: Expand AI Agents to explain MCP tools, skills, and AIBlock, preferably with a table.
directive_events.reason: Owner requested a more detailed AI Agents section after reviewing the current draft.

directive_events.owner_directive: Remove sections 6, 8, and 9, and remove Layer x prefixes from all section headings.
directive_events.reason: Owner simplified the chapter set and heading style while expanding AI Agents.

directive_events.owner_directive: Rename MCP tools to MCP server and state that SciStudio has 33 MCP tools.
directive_events.reason: Owner corrected the AI Agents terminology and requested an explicit MCP tool count.

directive_events.owner_directive: Rewrite the Plot System table for users: explain what it is, what it is for, its features, and why it is designed that way. Remove overly technical contract details.
directive_events.reason: Owner redirected Plot System from implementation contracts to a user-facing conceptual introduction.

directive_events.owner_directive: Add a chapter explaining the lineage system and reproducibility.
directive_events.reason: Owner added a new user-facing architecture topic after reviewing the AI and plot sections.

directive_events.owner_directive: Make the Plot System table use the same Surface and Role writing style as the other sections.
directive_events.reason: Owner aligned the Plot System table style with the rest of the user-facing architecture article.

directive_events.owner_directive: Remove section 1 Introduction. Directly tell readers to see the architecture document for full detail.
directive_events.reason: Owner removed the introductory chapter and simplified the opening.

directive_events.owner_directive: Open with: this is a user-facing overview, followed by the link for full architectural detail. Treat the length limit as non-blocking when it is only a default.
directive_events.reason: Owner clarified the exact opening tone and deprioritized the default length ceiling when readability benefits.

directive_events.owner_directive: Correct the Git and lineage description using the architecture document; the current Git positioning is factually inaccurate.
directive_events.reason: Owner identified an inaccurate simplification of the Git and lineage responsibility split; the canonical architecture section 4.6 defines the correct relationship.

directive_events.owner_directive: Position Git purely as convenient branching for workflow variants. Position lineage as the reproducibility system.
directive_events.reason: Owner corrected the user-facing product role of Git: branching convenience for parallel workflow variants, while lineage owns reproducibility.

docs_events.rationale: The canonical architecture contract is the source being summarized and does not change.
No product contract or behavior changes.
Documentation-only discoverability change with no release behavior change.
